name: NIKE, Inc.
summary: NIKE, Inc., incorporated in 1967 in Oregon, is the largest seller of athletic footwear and apparel in the world. The company designs, develops, markets and sells athletic footwear, apparel, equipment, accessories and services under the NIKE, Jordan and Converse brands. Products are sold through NIKE Direct operations (retail stores and digital platforms) and wholesale accounts including independent distributors, licensees and sales representatives in nearly all countries worldwide. Nearly all products are manufactured by independent contractors.

products.name: Athletic Footwear
products.description: Footwear designed primarily for specific athletic use, also worn for casual or leisure purposes, under the NIKE brand.

products.name: Sports Apparel
products.description: Athletic and casual apparel featuring NIKE trademarks, sold through same channels as footwear.

products.name: Performance Equipment and Accessories
products.description: Bags, socks, sport balls, eyewear, timepieces, digital devices, bats, gloves and protective equipment under the NIKE Brand.

products.name: Jordan Brand Products
products.description: Athletic and casual footwear, apparel and accessories focused on sport performance and streetwear using the Jumpman trademark.

products.name: Converse Products
products.description: Casual sneakers, apparel and accessories under Converse, Chuck Taylor, All Star, One Star, Star Chevron and Jack Purcell trademarks.

products.name: Fitness and Activity Apps
products.description: Sport, fitness and wellness apps and digital content offered as interactive consumer services.

products.name: Air Manufacturing Innovation Products
products.description: Small amounts of plastic products sold to other manufacturers through wholly-owned subsidiary Air Manufacturing Innovation.

products.name: Licensed Apparel
products.description: Apparel marketed with licensed college and professional team and league logos.

partnerships.description: Nearly all NIKE footwear and apparel products are manufactured by independent contractors outside the United States.
License agreements permit unaffiliated parties to manufacture and sell apparel, digital devices and sports equipment using NIKE-owned trademarks.
NIKE markets apparel bearing licensed college and professional team and league logos.
